senses.definition: to desire something to occur or to be true even though it is improbable or not possible

examples.x: He regularly wishes he could travel the world.
They wished they had invested in the stock market years ago.
I wish I could fly like a bird and see the world from above.
I can’t help but wish my dog could speak, even though I know it’s a fantasy.
Regretting his decision, he wished he could turn back time.

senses.definition: the action or process of reaching a particular thing

examples.x: He beamed with pride as he recounted his achievement of climbing the highest peak in the country.
The young artist beamed with pride at the achievement of finally selling her first painting.
The team celebrated their achievement of breaking the company's sales record for the quarter.
With the achievement of financial independence, Sarah was finally able to pursue her dream of traveling the world.
The team celebrated their achievement together.

senses.definition: a specific, concrete objective that a person or group actively works toward, believing it to be realistically achievable

examples.x: His aim is to finish the marathon in under four hours.
His ultimate aim remains securing a leadership role in his firm.
The scientists worked with the aim of developing a cost-effective vaccine.
After months of preparation, her aim of completing the course with top marks felt within reach.
Her aim is to pass the entrance exam on her first attempt.

senses.definition: to intend or attempt to achieve something

examples.x: She aimed to graduate with honors and put in countless hours studying.
The company is aiming to expand into new markets this year.
He aimed to impress the judges with his performance.
We aim to provide excellent customer service.

senses.definition: the will to obtain wealth, power, success, etc.

examples.x: Fueled by boundless ambition, the young entrepreneur launched her tech startup despite the crowded market.
The seasoned athlete's unwavering ambition kept her pushing for one more Olympic medal.
Although her initial artistic ambitions were met with skepticism, her dedication eventually earned her critical acclaim.
His ambition led him to train rigorously for the championship.
The scientist’s ambition to make groundbreaking discoveries fueled his research.

senses.definition: (of someone or something's future) likely to be successful and very good

examples.x: With his talent and dedication, his future in the company looks bright.
The young athlete's bright future in professional sports was obvious to everyone.
After securing a major investor, the startup's prospects became much brighter.
Her academic achievements suggest a bright future in medicine.
The economy is recovering, and the outlook for new businesses is bright.

senses.definition: achieving the intended or desired result

examples.x: The effective marketing campaign boosted sales significantly.
His effective communication skills allowed him to resolve conflicts peacefully.
Taking medication at the right dosage and time is essential for it to be effective.
The coach's effective strategies improved the team's performance on the field.
Wearing sunscreen every day is an effective way to protect your skin from sun damage.

senses.definition: an attempt to do something, particularly something demanding

examples.x: Despite her best effort, she couldn't solve the puzzle.
The team put in a tremendous effort to complete the project ahead of schedule.
Learning a new language requires persistent effort and practice.
His effort to climb the mountain was rewarded with a breathtaking view from the summit.
The rescue team made every effort to locate the missing hikers before nightfall.

senses.definition: a feeling of desire for something or of wanting something to happen
to do something difficult successfully

examples.x: She managed to finish the project just before the deadline.
He managed a quick glance before turning away.
The athlete managed to cross the finish line even with an injury.
He tried to speak, but managed only a few broken words.
Not only did he manage to meet the expectations, but he also exceeded them.
They couldn't have managed without my help.
Did he manage to find a solution to the problem after all?
Despite the pressure, she managed to stay calm.
The athlete had to manage the intense training schedule to peak at the right time for the competition.
She managed a polite laugh even though the joke was awkward.
She was too tired to manage the long hike alone.

senses.definition: (of a method, idea, or plan) likely to be successful or effective

examples.x: The engineer proposed a practical solution to the problem.
Her practical approach to time management helped her stay organized.
The team developed a practical plan to improve productivity.
The new policy is both practical and easy to implement.
She offered a practical solution to the problem that could be implemented immediately.

senses.definition: achieving success or progress

examples.x: The company experienced positive growth after implementing new strategies.
Regular exercise has a positive impact on overall health and well-being.
The feedback from customers was overwhelmingly positive, boosting morale among the staff.
Despite initial setbacks, the project eventually had a positive outcome.
The positive impact of volunteering was felt throughout the community.

senses.definition: having an unpleasant or harmful effect on someone or something

examples.x: The report highlighted the negative impact of pollution on wildlife.
The negative portrayal of certain communities in the media can perpetuate harmful stereotypes.
The test result came back negative, which was a relief.
Consuming excessive amounts of sugary drinks can have negative effects on overall health.
The movie received mixed reviews, with many pointing out its negative elements.

senses.definition: to help or support the progress or development of something

examples.x: The manager worked to promote teamwork and collaboration within the team.
The mentor played a crucial role in promoting the career development of the mentee.
The government launched a campaign to promote environmental conservation and sustainability.
The community members joined hands to promote local businesses and economic growth.

senses.definition: admiration for someone or something because of their achievements, qualities, etc.
to admire someone because of their achievements, qualities, etc.

examples.x: I respect my grandfather for his wisdom and life experiences.
Students are expected to respect their teachers by listening attentively and following classroom rules.
She respects her mentor and admires his dedication and integrity.
The students respect their teacher for her passion and commitment to education.
He respects his coach for his leadership and guidance on and off the field.

senses.definition: the most effective or proven method of achieving something

examples.x: Careful planning is the secret to success in this complex project.
Her grandmother's secret for baking the most delicious cookies was adding a pinch of cinnamon.
His secret to staying fit and healthy was exercising regularly and eating a balanced diet.

senses.definition: to provide someone or something with encouragement or help

examples.x: Friends and family members often support each other during challenging times.
While I was going through a tough time, they were supporting me unconditionally.
The government launched new programs to support small businesses during the economic downturn.
Friends and colleagues organized a fundraiser to support him in paying for his medical bills.
The teacher always tries to support her students by offering extra help after class.

senses.definition: to focus one's effort, time, or attention on something in order to achieve a particular goal

examples.x: She's working on a solution to address the recurring issues in the system.
The team is working on strategies to increase customer satisfaction.
They're working on a plan to boost efficiency in the workplace.

senses.definition: to think about something that one desires very much

examples.x: I dream of traveling the world and experiencing different cultures.
She dreams about a successful career in music.
He dreams of creating a sustainable and eco-friendly business.
The student dreams of graduating with honors and pursuing further studies.
We often dream about achieving our goals and aspirations.

senses.definition: (of a person) having a positive attitude and believing that good things are likely to happen

examples.x: The young artist felt hopeful after receiving positive feedback on her latest work.
Despite several rejections, the hopeful writer continued to submit her manuscript, believing in its potential.
The therapist encouraged the patient to remain hopeful, reminding them that progress takes time.
The hopeful politician delivered a speech brimming with optimism, inspiring the nation to work for a better future.

senses.definition: gradual movement toward a goal or a desired state

examples.x: The scientist made significant progress in her research on renewable energy.
With consistent practice, the student made steady progress on their guitar skills, mastering new chords each week.
Despite setbacks, the research team remained committed to their project and continued to make progress in finding a cure.
The patient showed slow but steady progress in his physical therapy.

senses.definition: to have something in mind as a plan or purpose

examples.x: She intends to travel abroad next summer.
He intends to pursue a career in medicine.
They intend to save money for a down payment on a house.
The company intends to launch a new product line next year.
I intend to start exercising regularly to improve my health.

senses.definition: to progress in a particular way

examples.x: Despite the initial setbacks, the project is going smoothly now.
The negotiations are going positively, and an agreement is within reach.
The concert went better than expected, with the audience cheering for an encore.
The business meeting went smoothly.
How did your presentation go?

senses.definition: the will, energy, and strength to keep trying to achieve or prevent something

examples.x: Despite facing numerous setbacks, her fight to achieve her dream of becoming a doctor never wavered.
He showed incredible fight in his battle against cancer, refusing to let the illness dictate his life.
The activist's lifelong fight for social justice has inspired many.
The athlete never gave up, her fight to win fueling her performance until the very end.

senses.definition: used to express that something positive or favorable has happened or is happening by chance

examples.x: Fortunately, the weather cleared up just in time for the outdoor event, allowing it to proceed smoothly.
She forgot her wallet at home, but fortunately, a friend was able to lend her some money for lunch.
The car broke down on the highway, but fortunately, a passing motorist stopped to help with repairs.
Fortunately, the fire alarm went off in time, and everyone safely evacuated the building before any harm occurred.
He misplaced his keys, but fortunately, he had a spare set stored in a secure location.

senses.definition: used to express regret or say that something is disappointing or sad

examples.x: Unfortunately, the flight was delayed due to severe weather conditions, causing inconvenience for the passengers.
She worked hard on the project, but unfortunately, it did not meet the client's expectations.
Unfortunately, the concert was canceled at the last minute, disappointing fans who had eagerly anticipated the event.
The team played well, but unfortunately, they lost the championship match by a narrow margin.
Unfortunately, the company had to downsize, resulting in the layoff of several employees.

senses.definition: in a manner that achieves what is desired or expected

examples.x: After months of hard work and dedication, she successfully completed her research project.
The team collaborated effectively and successfully launched the new product ahead of schedule.
Despite initial challenges, the negotiations were handled diplomatically and successfully reached a resolution.
He applied for the job and successfully secured a position with the company.
The students worked together on the group project and were able to present it successfully to their peers and instructors.

senses.definition: to directly cause something
to reach or gain a particular thing, typically requiring significant amount of effort

examples.x: After months of negotiations, they finally secured a contract with the new client.
She worked tirelessly to secure a scholarship for her studies abroad.
The team secured a victory in the final minutes of the game with a last-minute goal.
He secured a promotion at work after demonstrating his leadership skills and dedication.
Despite fierce competition, she secured a spot in the prestigious art exhibition.
